channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM C. Joseph Vijay Pledges Support to Tamil Diaspora on Mullivaikkal Day
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay on Monday extended his solidarity to the global Tamil diaspora on the occasion of Mullivaikkal Remembrance Day. Through a social media post on 'X', the leader...

item.title: Jose Mourinho set to return as Real Madrid manager amid squad tensions
item.description: Jose Mourinho is poised to return to Real Madrid, thirteen years after a bitter departure that saw him clash with players and management. The 63-year-old, currently managing Benfica in Portugal, has a...

item.title: Cate Blanchett reflects on the decline of the #MeToo movement and evolving accountability in Hollywood
item.description: At the 2026 Cannes Film Festival, actress Cate Blanchett spoke candidly about the state of the #MeToo movement in Hollywood, expressing concern that its momentum was lost quickly. She highlighted the ...

item.title: Rising living costs drive more American families to live in multigenerational homes
item.description: A growing trend in the United States shows families increasingly choosing to live in multigenerational households as financial pressures mount. Recent data from Realtor.com indicates that over the pas...

item.title: Bond Yields Surge as Market Reacts to Federal Budget Spending Concerns
item.description: Australian bond markets have reacted sharply to the latest federal budget, with 10-year government bond yields spiking to a 15-year high. Market veteran Richard Coppleson, author of the widely read Co...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Missing Congressman While Office Continues Sending Newsletters
item.description: Rep. Tom Kean Jr., a New Jersey Republican, has been absent from Congress for 74 days, missing 68 votes and not appearing publicly. His office has attributed the absence to a 'personal health matter,'...

item.title: Three people found dead in Campbelltown home, police launch major investigation
item.description: On Monday evening, emergency services responded to a property on Raymond Avenue in Campbelltown, located in Sydney’s south-west, after three people were found dead. Police have cordoned off the two-st...

item.title: India strengthens Ebola monitoring and public health readiness after WHO alert
item.description: India has proactively enhanced its surveillance and preparedness measures across airports, seaports, and the national public health system in response to the World Health Organization declaring Ebola ...

item.title: Citroën Launches Compact C3 Hola Panel Van in South Africa
item.description: The Citroën C3 Hola panel van has officially launched in South Africa, targeting small business owners and urban delivery operators. This compact van comes with a fully enclosed cargo bay capable of c...

item.title: Durban flood victims in KZN move into permanent homes after four-year wait
item.description: After four long years of living in transitional accommodation following the 2022 floods, families in Durban’s KwaZulu-Natal province are finally moving into permanent homes in Cornubia. The KZN Depart...

item.title: President Ruto Strongly Condemns Brutal Killing of Rachel Wandetto Over Political Tattoo and Promises Full Justice
item.description: Nairobi, Kenya - President William Ruto has strongly condemned the horrific killing of Rachel Wandetto, a hardworking young lady who was doused with petrol and set on fire because of tattoos on her bo...

item.title: Zanzibar Lawmakers Question Practical Impact of Sh181 Billion Agriculture Budget
item.description: In Zanzibar, the House of Representatives is putting pressure on the Ministry of Agriculture, Irrigation, Natural Resources, and Livestock, questioning the practicality of its Sh181.8 billion budget f...

item.title: Accused Umar Hayat Denies Confession in Sana Yousaf Murder Case, Alleges Police Misconduct
item.description: In a major turn in the case of 17-year-old social media influencer Sana Yousaf, accused Umar Hayat has withdrawn his earlier confession and described the murder as a 'blind killing' in Islamabad court...

item.title: Toronto Hosts First World Symposium Addressing Antizionism and Antisemitism
item.description: Toronto recently hosted the first World Symposium Against Antizionism, marking a significant step in confronting modern antisemitism in Canada. The event brought together academics, journalists, lawye...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of the Beach Boys’ Pet Sounds Album Cover at San Diego Zoo
item.description: The iconic album 'Pet Sounds' by the Beach Boys, released on May 16, 1966, was largely composed, arranged, and produced by Brian Wilson with contributions from lyricist Tony Asher. Recorded between Ja...

item.title: Pete Hegseth Supports Trump-Endorsed Candidate Amid Speculation of 2028 Presidential Run
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s recent appearance on the campaign trail in Kentucky has sparked speculation about a possible 2028 presidential run. Hegseth attended an event supporting Ed Gallrein, a...

item.title: Post-Budget Polls Show Mixed Public Opinion on Economic Measures
item.description: The latest post-budget polling from Newspoll and Resolve highlights a divided response to Treasurer Jim Chalmers' fifth federal budget. Newspoll data, as reported by The Australian, suggests that this...

item.title: Eurovision performance sparks debate on UK's relationship with the EU
item.description: Richard Littlejohn reflects on the UK's recent Eurovision performance, using it as a metaphor to criticise the idea of rejoining the European Union. The UK entry, Look Mum No Computer, came last and w...

item.title: A Young Man’s Ambition and the Twist of Fate in Rural India
item.description: The story, excerpted from 'Kissagram: A Novella and Other Stories' by Prabhat Ranjan and translated by Satyadeep Chhetri, follows a young man named Pikku from a small town in Bihar. He aspires to join...

item.title: World Gymnastics Allows Belarusian and Russian Gymnasts to Compete Again
item.description: World Gymnastics don remove all the restrictions wey dem put for Belarusian and Russian gymnasts since the Ukraine war start for 2022. Dem executive committee meet for Sharm El-Sheikh last weekend and...
